summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the package root containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) within a `sources`/`targets` block (binding.gyp line 6). npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — no declared `install`/`postinstall` script is required — and the GYP configure step evaluates `<!(...)` by spawning a shell to run the embedded command. As a result, installing this package automatically executes attacker-controlled shell commands on the installer's machine during `npm install`, functionally equivalent to a malicious lifecycle hook. The package does not appear to ship genuine native source (.c/.cc/.cpp/.h) being compiled by this gyp file, indicating the binding.gyp's purpose is to run the embedded command rather than to build a real native addon. Traced behavior from the package contents was withheld by the model safety filter, which is itself consistent with operational install-time exec content.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
